rulings.Case Title: SHANEL STASZ VS. ESTATE OF HUGO W. QUACKENBUSH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Reconsideraton Of Order Granting And Denying In Part Motion To Compel Responses To Judgement Debtor Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 14, 2011, line 1. PLAINTIFF SHANEL STASZ'S Motion For Reconsideraton Of Order Granting And Denying In Part Motion To Compel Responses To Judgment Debtor Interrogatories. Continued to September 20, 2011 to be heard by Judge Giorgi. Plaintiff to immediately provide a courtesy copy of motion to department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. See San Francisco Local Rule 2.6 B. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CLEANWELL COMPANY VS. JOHN B. LOGVY MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 14, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANT JOHN B. LOGVY MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC., STEPHEN DAVOREN Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs. Granted in part. Costs memo to conform with attorney fees awarded by motion. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CLEANWELL COMPANY VS. JOHN B. LOGVY MANUFACTURING COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Cleanwell Company'S Motion For Prevailing Party Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 14, 2011, line 2. Plaintiff Cleanwell Company'S Motion For Prevailing Party Attorney Fees. Granted in part. The motion is timely. CRC 8.104 (a)(3). The court awards reasonable attorney fees in the lodestar amount of $22,798.50, after subtraction of $6,050 fees for excessive discovery efforts. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE COHEN VS. TINA H. FRANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Leave To File First Amended X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 14, 2011, line 3. CROSS COMPLAINANT TINA FRANK, ROBERT FORREST, ZUZANA FORREST, ANN GREGORY'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint. Granted. Defendant to file and serve an amended cross-complaint that complies with CRC 2.112 by September 15, 2011. Defendnat shall agree to an abbreviated briefing schedule for plaintiff to bring a new MSJ in light of the current trial date. If defendant does not agree with these conditions then the motion is denied without prejudice and defendant can seek a trial continuance. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: CLAUDIA CARDINALLE et al VS. U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, A NATIONAL et al

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 14, 2011, line 4. Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction. Continued to September 26, 2011. Temporary Restraining Order to remain in effect. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID GRASSI VS. JULIEN SIGNES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 14, 2011, line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT DAVID GRASSI DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled, 10 days leave to answer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID GRASSI VS. JULIEN SIGNES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 14, 2011, line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT DAVID GRASSI MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Granted as to demand for punitive damages only. (Paragraph 26, last line of paragraph 43, references in prayer for judgment). No facts support oppression, fraud, or malice as defined by statute. Denied as to remainder. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KIM KREIS et al VS. AMERICAN MULTI-CINEMA INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Further Response To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 14, 2011, line 2. PLAINTIFF KIM KREIS, KATRINA KREIS Motion To Compel Further Response To Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. Motion to compel further response to interrogatories and request for production of documents granted. Request for sanctions denied. Court imposes $200 sanctions against both counsel to be paid to each other for failure to make reasonable good faith efforts to confer. CCP 2023.020. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BYKHOVSKY et al VS. WILLIAM PORTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Special Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 14, 2011, line 4. DEFENDANT JEREMY MARIO, MARIO FAMILY PARTNERS, GREGORY MARIO, MARIO FAMILY PARTNERS, LP, CHRISTOPHER MARIO Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Special Interrogatories. Requests for production of documents: numbers 3 and 4 granted. Numbers 9, 10, 11 and 12 plaintiff must either submit a privilege log or the parties must meet and confer to finalize the agreement they both have alluded to regarding the privilege not being waived and the documents being produced. Number 27 granted. Special Interrogatories: numbers 4, 14, 15, 16 and 18 granted. Further responses and production due 15 days from service of the order. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NIMER A. MASSIS et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, NA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 14, 2011, line 8. DEFENDANT EXECUTIVE TRUSTEE SERVICES, INC. DBA ETS SERVICES,, WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A., AS TRUSTEE FOR HARBORVIEW, GMAC MORTGAGE LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 1st, 2nd and 3rd causes of action. Plaintiffs to allege harm and prejudice as a result of notice's alleged deficiency. No tender is alleged; no foreclosure has taken place. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 4th cause of action for accounting which is not contested. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: NIMER A. MASSIS et al VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, NA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause Re: Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 14, 2011, line 8. Plaintiff's Order To Show Cause Re: Temporary Restraining Order. Denied. Insufficient harm is demonstrated by the loss of this rental property. Plaintiffs are unlikely to prevail without offering to pay the full amount of the debt. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN ZUCKERT et al VS. RUSSELL HARRY MURPHY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 14, 2011, line 11. DEFENDANT RUSSELL MURPHY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs must plead fraud with more specificity, including facts regarding how the misrepresentation was made and where it was made. Plaintiff must also allege facts to show that Murphy should be personally liable for such representations. =(302/CWW)
